questions.question:
A relation \(R\) is said to be circular if \(aRb\) and \(bRc\) together imply \(cRa\).
Which of the following options is/are correct?

(GATE CSE 2021 SET-1)
options:
A.

If a relation S is reflexive and symmetric, then S is an equivalence relation.

B.

If a relation S is circular and symmetric, then S is an equivalence relation.

C.

If a relation S is reflexive and circular, then S is an equivalence relation.

D.

If a relation S is transitive and circular, then S is an equivalence relation.
correct_answer:
C.

If a relation S is reflexive and circular, then S is an equivalence relation.
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Let \(R\) be the set of all binary relations on the set {1,2,3}. Suppose a relation is chosen from \(R\) at random. The probability that the chosen relation is reflexive (round off to 3 decimal places) is ______.

(GATE CSE 2020)

questions.question:
Let G be an arbitrary group. Consider the following relations on G:

R1: \(\forall a,b \in G, aR_1b\) if and only if \(\exists g \in G\) such that \(a=g^{-1}bg\)
R2: \(\forall a,b \in G, aR_2b\) if and only if \(a=b^{-1}\)

Which of the above is/are equivalence relation/relations?

(GATE CSE 2019)

questions.question:
A binary relation R on \(\mathbb{N}\times \mathbb{N}\) is defined as follows: (a,b)R(c,d) if \(a \leq c\) or \(b \leq d\). Consider the following propositions:
P: R is reflexive
Q: R is transitive
Which one of the following statements is TRUE?

(GATE CSE 2016 SET-2)
options:
B.

P is true and Q is false.

C.

P is false and Q is true
questions.correct_answer:
B.

P is true and Q is false.

questions.question:
Let R be a relation on the set of ordered pairs of positive integers such that \(((p,q),(r,s)) \in R\) if and only if \(p-s=q-r\). Which one of the following is true about R?

(GATE CSE 2015 SET-3)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Let R be the relation on the set of positive integers such that aRb if and only if a and b are distinct and have a common divisor other than 1. Which one of the following statements about R is true?

(GATE CSE 2015 SET-2)
options:
A.

R is symmetric and reflexive but not transitive

B.

R is reflexive but not symmetric and not transitive

C.

R is transitive but not reflexive and not symmetric

D.

R is symmetric but not reflexive and not transitive
questions.correct_answer:
D.

R is symmetric but not reflexive and not transitive

questions.question:
A relation R is defined on ordered pairs of integers as follows:
(x,y)R(u,v) if x\(\lt\)u and y\(\gt\)v. Then R is

(GATE CSE 2006)
options:
A.

Neither a Partial Order nor an Equivalence Relation

B.

A Partial Order but not a Total Order
questions.correct_answer:

Neither a Partial Order nor an Equivalence Relation

questions.question:
Let R and S be any two equivalence relations on a non-empty set A. Which one of the following statements is TRUE?

(GATE CSE 2005)
options:
A.

\(R\cup S,R\cap S\) are both equivalence relations.

D.

Neither \(R\cup S\) nor \(R\cap S\) is an equivalence relation.

questions.question:
Let \(R_{1}\) be a relation from \(A =\left \{ 1,3,5,7 \right \}\) to \(B = \left \{ 2,4,6,8 \right \}. R_{2}\) be another relation from B to C = {1, 2, 3, 4} as defined below:

i. An element x in A is related to an element y in B (under \(R_{1}\)) if x + y is divisible by 3.
ii. An element x in B is related to an element y in C (under \(R_{2}\)) if x + y is even but not divisible by 3.

Which is the composite relation \(R_{1}R_{2}\) from A to C?

(GATE IT 2004)

questions.question:
A relation R is defined on the set of integers as xRy iff (x + y) is even. Which of the following statements is true?

(GATE CSE 2000)
options:
B.

R is an equivalence relation having 1 equivalence class

C.

R is an equivalence relation having 2 equivalence classes

D.

R is an equivalence relation having 3 equivalence classes
questions.correct_answer:
C.

R is an equivalence relation having 2 equivalence classes
